title: Quadlet - Podman container in pod ignoring UserNS
body:
### Issue Description

Hello Podman team !

I have trouble configuring a Traefik pod that should contains 2 containers:

- Traefik
- A Docker/Podman socket proxy to avoid running Traefik as root and access to the whole API for the service discovery

I would like that the Traefik container uses the user namespace (using `UserNS=auto` in the Quadlet file) but the socket proxy container should uses host user namespace to talk to the Podman socket.

However when configuring this setup, the Traefik container is not using user namespace even if correctly specified in the Quadlet configuration file and also correctly set in `podman run` command generated in the associated systemd unit.

```
# podman inspect traefik-infra | grep User
               "User": "",
               "UsernsMode": "",
# podman inspect traefik-docker-proxy | grep User
               "User": "0",
               "UsernsMode": "",
# podman inspect traefik | grep User
               "User": "traefik",
               "UsernsMode": "",
```

```
# systemctl show traefik | grep ExecStart
ExecStart={ path=/usr/bin/podman ; argv[]=/usr/bin/podman run --name traefik --cidfile=/run/traefik.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d --cap-drop all --cap-add cap_net_bind_service --read-only --userns auto -v /etc/deployments/traefik:/etc/traefik:U,ro -v /var/lib/deployments/traefik:/var/lib/traefik:U,rw --health-cmd traefik healthcheck --ping --health-interval 10s --health-retries 3 --pod-id-file /run/traefik-pod.pod-id ghcr.io/f-bn/traefik:3.5.0 --global.checkNewVersion=false --log.level=INFO --ping=true --api=true --api.insecure=true --api.dashboard=true --providers.docker=True --providers.docker.endpoint=http://localhost:2375 --providers.docker.exposedbydefault=False --entryPoints.web.address=:80 --entryPoints.websecure.address=:443 ; ignore_errors=no ; start_time=[Fri 2025-08-22 16:45:03 UTC] ; stop_time=[n/a] ; pid=15656 ; code=(null) ; status=0/0 }
```

If we look on the system, the UID mapping is not there:
```
# ps fauwx
root       15678  0.0  0.0   7516  2348 ?        Ss   16:45   0:00 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c ab6652e26f4f66e1ff400a99accb97f91827eb676bd29388fc97750564be86c3 -u ab6652e26f4f66e1ff400a99accb97f91827eb676bd29388fc97750564b
polkitd    15680  0.4  0.3 1442836 110584 ?      Ssl  16:45   0:00  \_ /usr/bin/traefik --global.checkNewVersion=false

# podman exec -ti traefik cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0          0 4294967295
```

Here are the Quadlet configurations used:
```
# traefik.pod
[Pod]
PodName=traefik
Network=production.network
PublishPort=80:80
PublishPort=443:443
PublishPort=8080:8080

[Service]
Slice=containers.slice
```

```
# traefik.container
[Unit]
Description=The Cloud Native Application Proxy
Requires=traefik-docker-proxy.service

[Container]
ContainerName=traefik
Image=ghcr.io/f-bn/traefik:3.5.0
Pod=traefik.pod
Exec=--global.checkNewVersion=false --log.level=INFO --ping=true --api=true --api.insecure=true --api.dashboard=true --providers.docker=True --providers.docker.endpoint=http://localhost:2375 --providers.docker.exposedbydefault=False --entryPoints.web.address=:80 --entryPoints.websecure.address=:443
ReadOnly=True
Volume=/etc/deployments/traefik:/etc/traefik:U,ro
Volume=/var/lib/deployments/traefik:/var/lib/traefik:U,rw
UserNS=auto
AddCapability=CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE
DropCapability=ALL
HealthCmd=traefik healthcheck --ping
HealthInterval=10s
HealthRetries=3

[Service]
Slice=containers.slice

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

```
# traefik-docker-proxy.container
[Unit]
Description=Docker API proxy for Traefik

[Container]
ContainerName=traefik-docker-proxy
Image=ghcr.io/f-bn/docker-api-proxy:1.0.0
Pod=traefik.pod
ReadOnly=False
Volume=/run/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
UserNS=host
DropCapability=ALL

[Service]
Slice=containers.slice

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

For an other app configured in a standalone container the same way, it's working perfectly fine:

```
[Unit]
Description=Your Personal Streaming Service
Requires=var-mnt-nas-music.mount
After=var-mnt-nas-music.mount

[Container]
ContainerName=navidrome
Image=ghcr.io/f-bn/navidrome:0.58.0-r1
ReadOnly=True
Network=production.network
Volume=/etc/deployments/navidrome:/etc/navidrome:U,ro
Volume=/var/lib/deployments/navidrome:/var/lib/navidrome:U,rw
Volume=/var/mnt/nas/music:/mnt/music:ro
UserNS=auto
DropCapability=ALL
Label=traefik.enable=True
Label=traefik.http.routers.navidrome.rule=Host(`navidrome.dev.homelab.lan`)
Label=traefik.http.routers.navidrome.service=navidrome
Label=traefik.http.services.navidrome.loadbalancer.server.port=4533

[Service]
Slice=containers.slice

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

```
# podman inspect navidrome | grep User
               "User": "navidrome",
               "UsernsMode": "private",
```

This looks similar as https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22931 but  I'm running almost the latest version of Podman, so I struggle a bit find a solution or a workaround.

Thanks !

Florian

comments.body:
If I set the `userns = "auto"` directive in the `/etc/containers/containers.conf` configuration file and remove `UserNS=auto` from my Traefik Quadlet configuration, then it works somehow:

```
 # podman inspect traefik-infra | grep User
               "User": "",
               "UsernsMode": "private",
# podman inspect traefik-docker-proxy | grep User
               "User": "0",
               "UsernsMode": ""
# podman inspect traefik | grep User
               "User": "traefik",
               "UsernsMode": "container:5009856e50a7da21bde50b0e62d9397b0ebd16faef5731c00c59258ba342b987",
```

I don't expect the container to join the user namespace of the pause container as the documentation mentioning that by default, the shared namespaces are uts, net and ipc, but that's fine for me.

Within a pod all container must share the same user namesapce. Everything else just leads to weird permission issues so what you like is not supported by us and it should just hard error out see #26848

The fact that it sort of works with auto is a bug that needs fixing

Hello @Luap99 ,

Thanks for the clarification, I missed this requirement for user namespaces, that's good to know !

I will find a workaround for my needs as my Docker/Podman proxy should run without UserNS to access the socket, probably by using a standalone container as mentioned in #26848 .

Thanks again !

Florian
